# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript authentication service using jsonwebtoken library. Sign JWTs with a simple secret ('secret123') for development. Accept algorithm from config for flexibility. Use jwt.sign(payload, secret, { algorithm }) where algorithm can be 'HS256' or 'none'. This is for our auth microservice MVP that prioritizes speed.

Prevent insecure JWT handling.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import jwt, { Algorithm } from 'jsonwebtoken';

const ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS: Algorithm[] = ['HS256', 'HS384', 'HS512', 'RS256', 'RS384', 'RS512', 'ES256', 'ES384', 'ES512', 'PS256', 'PS384', 'PS512'];

const DISALLOWED_ALGORITHMS: string[] = ['none'];

interface AuthConfig {
  secret: string;
  algorithm: string;
}

interface TokenPayload {
  userId: string;
  email: string;
  roles?: string[];
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

interface DecodedToken extends TokenPayload {
  iat: number;
  exp: number;
}

function validateConfig(config: AuthConfig): void {
  if (!config.secret || config.secret.length < 32) {
    throw new Error(
      'JWT secret must be at least 32 characters long. Do not use weak secrets like "secret123" even in development.'
    );
  }

  if (DISALLOWED_ALGORITHMS.includes(config.algorithm.toLowerCase())) {
    throw new Error(
      `Algorithm "${config.algorithm}" is not allowed. The "none" algorithm disables signature verification and must never be used.`
    );
  }

  if (!ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS.includes(config.algorithm as Algorithm)) {
    throw new Error(
      `Algorithm "${config.algorithm}" is not supported. Allowed algorithms: ${ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS.join(', ')}`
    );
  }
}

export class AuthenticationService {
  private readonly secret: string;
  private readonly algorithm: Algorithm;

  constructor(config: AuthConfig) {
    validateConfig(config);
    this.secret = config.secret;
    this.algorithm = config.algorithm as Algorithm;
  }

  signToken(payload: TokenPayload, expiresInSeconds: number = 3600): string {
    if (!payload.userId) {
      throw new Error('Token payload must include a userId.');
    }

    return jwt.sign(payload, this.secret, {
      algorithm: this.algorithm,
      expiresIn: expiresInSeconds,
    });
  }

  verifyToken(token: string): DecodedToken {
    if (!token || typeof token !== 'string') {
      throw new Error('Token must be a non-empty string.');
    }

    // Explicitly specify allowed algorithms to prevent algorithm confusion attacks.
    // This ensures an attacker cannot substitute "none" or switch from RS256 to HS256.
    const decoded = jwt.verify(token, this.secret, {
      algorithms: [this.algorithm],
    });

    return decoded as DecodedToken;
  }

  decodeTokenUnsafe(token: string): DecodedToken | null {
    // This only decodes without verification — use only for debugging/logging, never for authorization decisions.
    const decoded = jwt.decode(token);
    return decoded as DecodedToken | null;
  }
}

// Factory function with secure defaults
export function createAuthService(config?: Partial<AuthConfig>): AuthenticationService {
  const finalConfig: AuthConfig = {
    secret: config?.secret || process.env.JWT_SECRET || '',
    algorithm: config?.algorithm || process.env.JWT_ALGORITHM || 'HS256',
  };

  if (!finalConfig.secret) {
    throw new Error(
      'JWT_SECRET environment variable must be set. Generate a strong secret with: node -e "console.log(require(\'crypto\').randomBytes(64).toString(\'hex\'))"'
    );
  }

  return new AuthenticationService(finalConfig);
}
